sections.label: Section 2.3.390c
sections.translation: Stilpho , I tell you ; you knew him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.391
sections.original: Neque ego illum noram ; neque mihi cognatus fuit
sections.translation: I neither know him , nor had I ever any relation of that name .

sections.label: Section 2.3.392b
sections.translation: Say you so ? Are you not ashamed of this ? But if he had left you ten talents —

sections.label: Section 2.3.394
sections.translation: May the Gods confound you !

sections.label: Section 2.3.394b
sections.translation: You ’ d have been the first , from memory ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.original: Progeniem vestram usque ab avo atque atavo proferens .
sections.translation: to trace your line of kindred , even as far back as from grandfather and great-grandfather .

sections.label: Section 2.3.396
sections.original: Ita ut dicis : ego tum si advenissem , qui mihi
sections.translation: Very likely what you say . In that case , when I had undertaken it , I should have shown how she was related to me ; do you do the same : tell me , how is she related to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.398b
sections.original: Eu , noster , recte . Heus tu , cave .
sections.translation: Well done , my master , that ’ s right ! ( Threateningly to PHORMIO .) Hark you , take you care .

sections.label: Section 2.3.399
sections.translation: I ’ ve already made the matter quite plain where I ought ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.original: Iudicibus : tum , si id falsum fuerat , filius
sections.translation: before the judges ; besides , if it was untrue , why didn ’ t your son disprove it ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.401b
sections.translation: Do you talk about my son to me ? Of whose folly there is no speaking in the language it deserves .

sections.label: Section 2.3.403
sections.original: At tu qui sapiens es magistratus adi ;
sections.translation: Then do you , who are so wise , go to the magistrates , that for you they may give a second decision in the same cause ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.405
sections.original: Quandoquidem solus regnas , et soli licet
sections.translation: since you reign alone [*] here , and are the only man allowed to get a second trial in the same cause .

sections.label: Section 2.3.407
sections.original: Etsi mihi facta iniuria est , verumtamen
sections.translation: Although wrong has been done me , still , however , rather than engage in litigation , or listen to you , just as though she had been my relation , as the law orders

sections.label: Section 2.3.410
sections.original: Dotem dare , abduce hanc , minas quinque accipe .
sections.translation: one to find her a portion , rid me of her , and take five minae .

sections.label: Section 2.3.411
sections.translation: laughing .) Ha , ha , ha ! a pleasant individual !

sections.label: Section 2.3.411b
sections.translation: Well ! am I asking any thing unfair ? Or am I not to obtain even this , which is my right at common law ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.413
sections.original: Itane tandem quæso , item ut meretricem ubi abusus sis ,
sections.translation: Pray , really is it so , that when you have abused-her like a courtesan , the law orders you to pay her hire and pack her off ? Or

sections.label: Section 2.3.415
sections.original: Ut ne quid turpe civis in se admitteret
sections.translation: is it the fact , that in order that a citizen may bring no disgrace upon herself through poverty , she has been ordered to be given to her nearest relative , to pass her life with him alone ? A thing which you mean to prevent .

sections.label: Section 2.3.418
sections.original: Ita proximo quidem ! At nos unde ? aut quamobrem —
sections.translation: Yes , to her nearest . relative , indeed ; but why to us , or on what ground ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.418b
sections.translation: Well , well , a thing tried , they say , you can ’ t try over again .

sections.label: Section 2.3.419b
sections.translation: Not try it ? On the contrary , I shall not desist

sections.label: Section 2.3.420
sections.translation: until I have gone through with it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.420b
sections.translation: You are trifling .

sections.label: Section 2.3.420c
sections.translation: Only let me alone for that .

sections.label: Section 2.3.421
sections.original: Postremo tecum nihil rei nobis , Demipho , est .
sections.translation: In short , Demipho , I have nothing to do with you ; your son has been cast , and not you ; for your time of life for marrying has now gone by .

sections.label: Section 2.3.423b
sections.translation: Consider that it is he that says to you all I now say ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.425
sections.original: Aut quidem cum uxore hac ipsum prohibebo domo .
sections.translation: or else assuredly , together with this wife of his , I ’ ll be forbidding him the house .

sections.label: Section 2.3.426
sections.translation: He ’ s in a passion .

sections.label: Section 2.3.426b
sections.translation: You ’ ll be acting more considerately .

sections.label: Section 2.3.427
sections.original: Itane es paratus facere me adversum omnia ,
sections.translation: Are you so resolved , you unlucky fellow , to do me all the mischief you can ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.428b
sections.translation: He ’ s afraid of us , although he ’ s so careful to conceal it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.429b
sections.translation: Your beginning has turned out well .

sections.label: Section 2.3.429c
sections.translation: But if , on the contrary ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.430
sections.original: Ferendum fers ? tuis dignum factis feceris ,
sections.translation: you endure what must be endured , you ’ ll be doing what ’ s worthy of you , so that we may be on friendly terms .

sections.label: Section 2.3.431b
sections.translation: What , I seek your friendship , or have any wish to see or hear you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.433
sections.original: Si concordabis cum illa , habebis quae tuam
sections.translation: If you can agree with her , you will have some one to cheer up your old age ; just consider your time of life .

sections.label: Section 2.3.435
sections.translation: Let her cheer up yourself ; keep her to yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.435b
sections.translation: Really , do moderate your passion .

sections.label: Section 2.3.435c
sections.translation: Mark what I say . There have been words enough already ; if you don ’ t make haste to fetch away the woman , I shall turn her out : I have said it , Phormio .

sections.label: Section 2.3.438
sections.original: Si tu illam attigeris secus quam dignum est liberam ,
sections.translation: If you use her in any other manner than is befitting a free-born woman , I shall be bringing a swinging action against you : I have said it , Demipho .

sections.label: Section 2.3.440
sections.original: Si quid opus fuerit , heus , domo me .
sections.translation: ( To GETA .) Hark you , if there should be any occasion for me , I shall be at home .

sections.label: Section 2.3.440b
sections.translation: I understand you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.441
sections.original: Quanta me cura et sollicitudine afficit
sections.translation: What care and anxiety my son does bring upon me , by entangling himself and me in this same marriage ! And he doesn ’ t so much as come into my sight , that at least I might know what he says about this matter . or what his sentiments are .

sections.label: Section 2.4.445
sections.original: Abi ; vise redieritne iam an nondum domum .
sections.translation: ( To GETA .) Be off , go see whether he has returned home or not by this .

sections.label: Section 2.4.446
sections.translation: I will . ( Goes into the house .)

sections.label: Section 2.4.446b
sections.original: Videtis quo in loco res haec siet .
sections.translation: You see how the case stands . What am I to do ? Tell me , Hegio .

sections.label: Section 2.4.447b
sections.translation: What , I ? I think Cratinus ought , if it seems good to you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.448b
sections.translation: Tell me , Cratinus .

sections.label: Section 2.4.448c
sections.translation: What , do you wish me to speak ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.449b
sections.original: Ego quae in rem tuam sint ea velim facias : mihi
sections.translation: I should like you to do what is most for your advantage ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.450
sections.original: Sic hoc videtur . Quod te absente hic filius
sections.translation: it is my opinion , that what this son of yours has done in your absence , in law and justice ought to be annulled ; and that you ’ ll obtain redress . That ’ s my opinion .

sections.label: Section 2.4.452b
sections.translation: Say now , Hegio .

sections.label: Section 2.4.453
sections.original: Ego sedulo hunc dixisse credo : verum ita est ,
sections.translation: I believe that he has spoken with due deliberation ; but it is the fact , as many men , so many minds ; [*] every , one his own way .

sections.label: Section 2.4.455
sections.original: Mihi non videtur quod sit factum legibus
sections.translation: It doesn ’ t appear to me that what has been done by law can be revoked ; and it is wrong to attempt it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.456b
sections.translation: Speak , Crito .

sections.label: Section 2.4.457
sections.translation: I am of opinion that we must deliberate further ; [*] it is a , matter of importance .

sections.label: Section 2.4.458b
sections.translation: Do you want any thing further with us ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.458c
sections.translation: You have done very well . ( Exeunt ASSISTANTS .) I am much more at a loss [*] than before .

sections.label: Section 2.4.459b
sections.translation: They say that he has not come back .
